channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Police bust restaurant serving ‘best-selling’ pizza with side of cocaine
item.description: German police have raided a Düsseldorf pizzeria for allegedly serving pies with a side of cocaine. The restaurant's 36-year-old manager was arrested.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korea warns it can send arms to Ukraine after reports of North's troops in Russia
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea warned Tuesday it could consider supplying weapons to Ukraine in response to North Korea allegedly dispatching troops to Russia, as both North Korea and Russia denied the movements. NATO's secretary general said that would mark a “significant escalation.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Walmart employee found dead inside walk-in oven at Canada store: police
item.description: Authorities in Canada are continuing an investigation into the death of a Walmart employee who was found inside a walk-in oven at the store.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Blinken urges Netanyahu to seek truces in Lebanon, Gaza
item.description: Secretary of State Antony J. Blinken met with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Tuesday to discuss ending the conflict in Lebanon and Gaza in addition to securing pathways for humanitarian aid, according to a readout from the meeting.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Iran Revolutionary Guard officer charged in plot to kill American author in US
item.description: The Justice Department unsealed an indictment against four Iranian officials Tuesday for their alleged involvement in a murder-for-hire targeting Iranian-American journalist Masih Alinejad.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Desperation amid search for survivors of an airstrike on a crowded area near major Beirut hospital
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Nearly 16 hours after an Israeli airstrike hit across the street from Beirut’s main public hospital, rescuers were still removing debris Tuesday from the overcrowded slum area.
item.dc:creator: SARAH EL DEEB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 19-year-old woman found dead in oven in Canada Walmart
item.description: A Canadian Walmart store was closed for a third day Tuesday as police investigated the death of a woman whose body was found inside an oven.
item.dc:creator: Wil Day, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A gasoline truck overturns and explodes near Uganda's capital, killing 11
item.description: Police in Uganda say least 11 people including children were killed when a fuel truck exploded next to a highway on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Rodney Muhumuza, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 2 transgender women stabbed to death at home in northwest Pakistan
item.description: PESHAWAR, Pakistan (AP) — Two men armed with daggers killed two transgender women at their home in conservative northwest Pakistan overnight before fleeing the scene, police said, a sign of increasing violence against trans people in the country.
item.dc:creator: RIAZ KHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: FBI says it's investigating unauthorized release of documents on Israel's possible attack plans
item.description: The FBI says it's investigating the unauthorized release of classified documents on Israel’s preparation for a potential retaliatory attack on Iran.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Peru’s ex-president Toledo gets more than 20 years in prison in case linked to corruption scandal
item.description: LIMA, Peru (AP) — Peru’s former President Alejandro Toledo on Monday was sentenced to 20 years and six months in prison in a case involving Brazilian construction giant Odebrecht, which became synonymous with corruption across Latin America, where it paid millions of dollars in bribes to government
item.dc:creator: FRANKLIN BRICEÑO and REGINA GARCIA CANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Missing student in Spain was likely crushed, incinerated after passing out in trash while partying: cops
item.description: A student missing in Majorca, Spain, was likely crushed to death in a garbage truck before she was incinerated at a waste disposal plant, police said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel says it killed a Hezbollah official expected to become the group's next leader
item.description: The Israeli military says one of its airstrikes in early October killed a top Hezbollah official who had been widely expected to become the group’s next leader.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Blinken heads to Middle East in wake of Sinwar's killing
item.description: Secretary of State Antony Blinken is going to the Middle East in the wake of the killing of Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Italian surfer dies in freak accident after swordfish impales her while surfing in Indonesia
item.description: An Italian surfer hunting for waves off the west coast of Sumatra died after she was stabbed in the chest by the bill of a swordfish, in what is being considered a freak accident.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel says more strikes are coming against a Hezbollah-run financial institution
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Israel said late Monday it planned to carry out more strikes in Lebanon against a Hezbollah-run financial institution that it targeted the night before and which it says uses customers' deposits to finance attacks against Israel.
item.dc:creator: SARAH EL DEEB and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korea demands withdrawal of North Korean troops allegedly helping Russia fight Ukraine
item.description: South Korea issued a demand to Russia to condemn the cooperation between Russia and North Korea, calling for the withdrawal of North Korean troops from Russia's war with Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Austin warns 'no silver bullet' to defeat Putin as US aid hangs in balance ahead of elections
item.description: Secretary of Defense Austin said there is "no silver bullet" to defeat Russian President Putin and urged the global community to bolster aid to Ukraine as concern mounts over continued U.S. support.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: American reportedly kidnapped in Philippine coastal town; police investigating
item.description: Police in the Philippines are searching for American national Elliot Onil Eastman, from Vermont, after receiving reports gunmen had kidnapped him.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Paul Whelan describes 'torture' of Russian prison
item.description: Former Marine Paul Whelan described the “torture” he endured while being held captive in a Russian prison for years. “The Russian government wanted to put pressure on the United States by treating me badly. Sleep deprivation is considered torture,” Whelan said Sunday on CBS’s “Face the Nation.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Reddit user discovers mysterious 'doorway' in Antarctica
item.description: "Monsters Inc." bedroom doors, and an underground bunker were more suggested explanations, some in jest others not.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: NHC monitoring Hurricane Oscar as it nears Cuba
item.description: Hurricane Oscar is being closely monitored by the National Hurricane Center as it makes its way toward Cuba on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Cuba gets some electricity back after major power outage left millions in the dark
item.description: HAVANA (AP) — Some electricity was restored in Cuba, the government said Saturday, after the island nation's worst blackout in at least two years left millions without electricity for two days.
item.dc:creator: ANDREA RODRIGUEZ, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Cuba working to reestablish electrical service after second grid collapse
item.description: Cuba's government is reportedly working to reestablish electrical service across the country after the national grid collapsed for the second time since midday Friday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Strike on Netanyahu home 'a signal' Iran proxies not incapacitated, expert says
item.description: A recent drone strike targeting the home of Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is sending "a signal" that Iran-backed militant groups are not "incapacitated," despite losing senior leadership in recent weeks, according to a Middle East expert. Daniel Levy, the president of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Harvard donations drop; alums cut ties over anti-Israel protests
item.description: Harvard, the nation's richest university, saw donations plunge 15% after campus protests, new report shows.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, The Hill

item.title: Israel says drone launched toward Netanyahu's home from Lebanon
item.description: The office of Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said early Saturday that a drone was launched from Lebanon toward Netanyahu's home in Caesarea. Netanyahu and his wife were not home at the time of the incident and no casualties were reported, according to The Associated Press (AP).
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Middle East latest: Hezbollah says its war with Israel is entering a new phase
item.description: Lebanon’s Hezbollah militant group said Friday it is entering a new phase in its fight against invading Israeli troops, as the region reckons with the killing of top Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar in a battle with Israeli forces in Gaza. Hamas acknowledged Sinwar’s death and described him as a martyr.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Cuba's power grid fails, plunging country into darkness
item.description: Cuba's government closed schools and non-essential industries and sent most state workers home in a last-ditch effort to keep the lights on for residents.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Two Venezuelan men shot in head in Juarez
item.description: Police in Juarez, Mexico, are investigating a shooting that left one Venezuelan dead, and another critically injured on Wednesday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation

item.title: US long-range B-2 stealth bombers target underground bunkers of Yemen's Houthi rebels
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — The U.S. military unleashed B-2 stealth bombers to target underground bunkers used by Yemen’s Houthi rebels early Thursday, a major escalation in the American response to the rebels' attacks on Mideast shipping lanes that appeared to be a warning to Iran as well.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: No slowing of US government contracts for Raytheon amid Qatar bribery charges
item.description: Under the agreement, the company will have to demonstrate good conduct for the next 3½ years and foster a culture of ethics and compliance with anti-corruption laws.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation staff, NewsNation
